market_scope.resolution_source: Station-linked source / Weather Underground style whole-degree daily high

ensemble.coverage_note: Ensemble in the updated archive is only available on a short trailing window and should be used only as a weak confirmation layer.

detection.rule: Default regime when day is neither clearly dry/clear nor clearly suppressed by cloud/rain.

adjustments.name: ecmwf_warmer_than_gfs
adjustments.reason: ECMWF adds limited upside confirmation when meaningfully warmer than GFS.

adjustments.name: clear_regime
adjustments.reason: Clear days in Paris tend to outperform raw model maxima.

adjustments.name: cloudy_regime
adjustments.reason: Cloudy days strongly cap the daily high.

adjustments.name: rainy_regime
adjustments.reason: Rainy days reduce realized upside but not always catastrophically.

adjustments.name: obs10_above_gfs10
adjustments.reason: Observed early warmth usually implies further outperformance.

adjustments.name: obs10_below_gfs10
adjustments.reason: Observed lag relative to GFS usually implies reduced realized high.

adjustments.name: noon_underperformance
adjustments.reason: By noon, Paris usually cannot recover a large shortfall.

adjustments.name: after_14_no_new_high_and_cooling
adjustments.reason: After the main peak window, a cooling path should decay higher buckets.

intraday_path_rules.max_by_noon_rule: In most Paris days, only 0-1C remains after noon; 2C is possible; 3C+ is rare.

rain_after_high_rule.effect: Lock current high as primary exact bucket and sharply reduce higher bucket probabilities.

sticky_high_rule.effect: Increase confidence that the current high is the final high.

no_new_high_after_14utc_rule.condition: time >= 14 UTC and no new high for at least one hour
no_new_high_after_14utc_rule.effect: Discount the next higher bucket aggressively.

trading_rules.post_14utc_market_scope: Only trade the current observed high bucket and its nearest neighbor.
trading_rules.skip_condition: If absolute edge is below threshold, do not trade.

scenario_invalidation.condition: fresh_METAR moves at least 1C against the scenario

path_break.condition: expected clear acceleration fails and rainy/cloudy suppression appears

post_peak_confirmation.condition: current_high confirmed by cooling/rain after peak

warnings: Do not trust ECMWF literally on warm clear Paris days; it often underpredicts the final high.
Do not extrapolate a clear-day afternoon ramp onto rainy or heavily clouded days.
After noon, assume limited upside unless there is obvious active acceleration.
After 14-15 UTC, no new high usually means rapid upper-tail decay.
Use ensemble only as a weak confirmation layer for Paris in this data set.

operational_summary.primary_city_filter: Morning sky regime and rain presence
operational_summary.main_post_noon_rule: Paris usually has only 0-1C of upside left after noon.
operational_summary.main_post_peak_rule: Once a new high is followed by rain or cooling, treat that high as the most likely final high.
